originalTitle: Police fire shots in air to disperse angry crowds at DR Congo Ebola treatment centre
title: Helping Friends in DR Congo Stay Healthy!
content:
Hello, wonderful friends! I'm your friendly news reporter from our amazing continent, Africa! I am here to tell you an important story. Today, we are learning about staying safe and helping each other in a country called DR Congo. It's a big, beautiful country right here in Africa! It is so important to learn about things like health. When we learn, we can all be kind and smart helpers! We can make a difference and keep our communities strong and happy.

What is Happening in DR Congo?

Imagine a big country in Africa, with lots of green trees and flowing rivers. This country is called the Democratic Republic of Congo. We often call it DR Congo for short. Sometimes, people in DR Congo get a special kind of sickness. Doctors and nurses are working super, super hard every single day to help everyone feel better. When someone they love gets sick, people can feel very, very sad and worried. They might not understand all the special rules that doctors have. This can make them feel upset or confused. But guess what? Everyone wants the same thing! Both the doctors and the families want to keep everyone safe and healthy. They all want to stop the sickness from spreading.

What is Ebola? A Little Sickness Story

Ebola is a sickness. It's like a very, very bad flu or a fever that makes people feel very unwell. It can make people feel very tired and have a really high temperature. This sickness is caused by tiny, tiny germs that we cannot see with our eyes. These germs are so small, you need a special microscope to spot them! These tiny germs can spread from one person to another. This happens if you touch someone who is sick. Or, the germs can spread if you touch things that a sick person has touched. That's why doctors and nurses need to be extra careful! They work hard to stop these sneaky germs. Did you know? Washing your hands with soap and water is a super-duper way to get rid of many germs! Try to wash your hands often. It's a great idea to wash them especially before you eat. And always wash your hands after you play outside or use the toilet. It's a simple trick to stay healthy!

Why Do Doctors Wear Special Suits?

Have you ever seen pictures of doctors wearing big, white suits? They might look a bit like brave astronauts ready for space! These special suits are very, very important for the doctors and nurses. They wear them to keep themselves safe from the tiny germs. The doctors wear these suits from their head all the way down to their toes. This means the germs cannot get on their skin or on their clothes. It helps them stay healthy so they can keep helping others who are sick. It's like wearing a superhero uniform to fight the bad germs! They are true heroes helping people every day. Did you know? These special suits are called Personal Protective Equipment. We can say 'PPE' for short! It's like a shield that protects the doctors and nurses from tiny germs. These shields are very strong and help keep our healthcare heroes safe.

Helping Our Friends Get Better

When someone gets sick with Ebola, they go to a special place. This place is called an Ebola treatment centre. It's like a special hospital just for this sickness. It’s a place where doctors and nurses know exactly how to help. In these centres, doctors and nurses give people very special care. They give them medicines and special drinks to help them feel better and get strong again. They work very hard, day and night, to help everyone get well. They are always there to help. It's a safe place where sick people can rest and get strong again. And it also helps stop the germs from spreading to other people in the village or town. This is super important because we want everyone to stay healthy and happy! It keeps the whole community safe.

Being Safe with Loved Ones Who Are Sick

When someone in a family is very sick, it makes everyone feel very, very sad. And when someone passes away, families feel an even bigger sadness. They want to say goodbye to their loved ones in their usual ways, following their traditions. This is a very natural feeling. But with Ebola, the tiny germs can still be on a body, even after someone has passed away. If people touch the body, the germs can spread to them too. This is why doctors have very special rules for funerals. These rules are not to be mean, but to be safe. The doctors need to help families bury their loved ones in a very safe way. This special way makes sure the germs cannot spread to anyone else in the family or the village. It's a way to keep everyone else safe and healthy. It can be hard for families to understand sometimes because they are so sad, but it is for everyone's safety and well-being. Did you know? It's important to listen to grown-ups and doctors when they tell us how to stay safe and healthy. They know the best ways to keep germs away and protect everyone around us! Always trust the people who want to keep you safe.

Why Working Together is So Important

Sometimes, people feel scared or angry. They might not understand why doctors have all these special rules. They might want to do things their own way, which is understandable when you are upset. But when everyone works together, we are much, much stronger! When families listen to the doctors, and doctors talk kindly and clearly to families, everyone can help stop the sickness. It’s like being on a team. Every single person on the team is important. Each person has a part to play. It's like when you play a fun game with your friends. If everyone works together, you can win the game! In DR Congo, everyone is trying to win against this sickness. They are working hard as a team to keep everyone healthy and safe. Teamwork makes the dream work!

Did You Know? Fun Facts to Stay Healthy!

* Did you know? Our bodies are amazing! They have tiny helpers inside called antibodies. These tiny helpers fight off germs and keep us strong. Eating healthy food like fruits and vegetables helps these helpers stay super strong! * Did you know? Sneezing and coughing can send germs into the air! Always try to sneeze or cough into your elbow. It's like making a vampire cape over your mouth! This helps keep germs from flying around and landing on others. * Did you know? Clean water is super important for everyone! Many people in Africa work very hard to make sure everyone has clean water to drink and wash with. Clean water helps us stay healthy and clean.

Let's All Be Heroes!

We can all be heroes in our own ways! We can listen to our parents, teachers, and other grown-ups who care for us. We can remember to wash our hands often with soap and water. We can be kind to everyone around us, even when things are difficult. And we can remember that doctors and nurses are working very, very hard to help people in DR Congo. They are real-life superheroes, helping people feel better and stopping germs from spreading! We wish them strength and success every day. Remember, by being smart and kind, we can help keep ourselves and our communities healthy and happy. Let's learn, let's help, and let's spread kindness, not germs! We can all make a difference in the world.
summary: In DR Congo, doctors and families are working together to keep everyone safe from a sickness called Ebola, learning to follow special rules to stop germs from spreading.
whyMatters: This story is important because it teaches us about helping people who are sick, listening to doctors, and working together as a community to stay healthy and safe from tiny germs.

title: Kenya police shake up president's protection team after security breach
content: A man managed to evade security and embrace William Ruto before being wrestled to the ground.

content: Pope Leo has warned that some AI-powered weapons systems are becoming ‘practically beyond human control'.

originalTitle: Trump links Iran negotiations to expansion of Abraham Accords
title: Making Friends and Talking for Peace Around the World!
content:
Hello, wonderful friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, coming to you live from our amazing continent of Africa! I have some super exciting news to share with all you smart boys and girls. It's all about making new friends and talking things out so everyone can live in peace. Let's dive in!

Who is Donald Trump?

Our story today is about a very important person named Mr. Donald Trump. He used to be the big boss, the President, of a country far away called America. America is a very big country with lots of people, just like some countries here in Africa. When someone is a President, they help make big decisions for their country and talk to leaders from other countries too. Mr. Trump often talked about how to make the world a safer and friendlier place. He wanted countries to get along, just like you want to get along with your friends at school or your brothers and sisters at home. It's always better when we are all friends, isn't it?

What are the Abraham Accords?

Now, let's talk about something really special called the 'Abraham Accords.' What are they? Imagine you and your friend had a little disagreement, but then you decide to talk and shake hands and be friends again. That's a bit like what the Abraham Accords are! These are special agreements, like big promises, that some countries made to be friends. They decided to stop disagreeing and start working together. It’s like saying, 'Let's be friends and play together!' The countries that made these amazing friendships were Israel, the United Arab Emirates (we call it UAE for short), Bahrain, Sudan, and Morocco. Yes, Sudan and Morocco are in our very own Africa! So, this news is extra special for us! These countries decided to talk, visit each other, and help each other. They started doing things like sharing ideas about farming, building new schools, and even flying planes to visit each other’s lands. Isn't that wonderful? When countries are friends, they can do so much good together. **Did you know?** The name 'Abraham Accords' comes from a very old and respected person named Abraham. Many people in these countries look up to him. It's like naming a friendship agreement after someone who taught us to be kind and welcoming to everyone!

Talking to Iran: Making Everyone Safe

Next, let’s talk about another country called Iran. Iran is also a very old and interesting country, with lots of history and beautiful places. Sometimes, countries have big problems or disagreements. When this happens, wise leaders try to 'negotiate.' What does 'negotiate' mean? It just means talking! It means sitting down, listening to each other, and trying to find a way to solve problems without any fighting. It's like when you talk to your friend to figure out who gets to play with the ball first, instead of grabbing it. Mr. Trump believes it's very important to talk to Iran. These talks, or negotiations, are usually about big things like making sure everyone feels safe and that countries don't make scary weapons. It's all about trying to make sure the world is a peaceful place for all of us, especially for you, the children, who are the future. Just like when you talk to your family about what to have for dinner, these big leaders talk about how to make the world better. **Did you know?** Talking is one of the best ways to solve any problem! When people talk with respect, they can understand each other better and find solutions that make everyone happy. It's like a superpower for peace!

How Do They Connect?

So, here's the clever part! Mr. Trump said that these two things – the 'Abraham Accords' (making new friends) and 'Iran negotiations' (talking to solve problems) – are connected. He thinks that if more countries become friends and make peace agreements, like the Abraham Accords, it will help other talks, like the ones with Iran, go even better. It’s like when one good thing happens, it can help other good things happen too! Imagine you help your friend with their homework, and then your friend feels happy and wants to help someone else. It's a bit like that! When countries see others becoming friends and working together, it shows them that peace is possible. It makes everyone think, 'Hey, maybe we can also talk and solve our problems too!' It creates a warm, friendly feeling that can spread across the world. Mr. Trump believes that more peace agreements can make the world more ready for good, helpful talks with countries like Iran. He hopes that this big friendship energy can make everyone want to talk and make good choices for safety and peace.

Why Does This Matter for Us in Africa?

This news is important for everyone, including all of us here in Africa. When countries make friends and talk instead of fight, it makes the whole world a safer place. When there is peace, countries can focus on helping their people. They can build more schools, like the one you go to. They can make sure everyone has healthy food to eat. They can build hospitals to help sick people get better. And they can even find better ways to get clean water to every home. When countries are friends, they can share good ideas. An idea that helps farmers grow more food in one country can be shared with another. A way to stop sickness that works in one place can be used somewhere else. This is how we all grow stronger and healthier together. Peace is like sunshine for the world – it helps everything good grow! **Did you know?** Africa is a continent with 54 amazing countries! When some African countries like Sudan and Morocco make peace agreements, it shows other countries that working together is super important for a brighter future for all children!

A Brighter Future Together!

So, my dear friends, the big message today is all about friendship and talking. It’s about how making more friends can help solve problems and make the whole world a happier and safer place for everyone. Let’s all remember to be friendly, talk things out, and help each other, just like these big countries are trying to do. Every little act of kindness and friendship helps build a more peaceful world. That's all for today from your friendly reporter! Keep shining bright!
summary: Former US President Donald Trump thinks that if more countries become friends, like in the Abraham Accords, it will help other countries, like Iran, talk to solve big problems for peace.
whyMatters: This is important because when countries are friends and talk nicely, the whole world becomes a safer and happier place, and everyone can help each other grow and learn more.

title: Australian Gaza aid flotilla activists allege abuse after Israeli abduction
content: Activists allege assaults and torture during Israeli detention after flotilla intercepted in international waters.

originalTitle: Deal with US not imminent, Iran says
title: Big Countries Talk, But Not So Fast!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hello, amazing kids! It’s your friendly African news reporter here, bringing you some exciting news from far, far away! Today, we’re going to talk about countries, and how they talk to each other. It's like when you and your friends want to play a game, and you need to agree on the rules! Sometimes, grown-up countries need to talk about important things too. They might talk about helping each other, or about sharing things, or just about being good neighbours. It's really important for countries to be good friends, just like it's important for us to be kind to our classmates. Did you know? The world has almost 200 different countries! Each one is like a big family with its own special people and culture. Isn't that amazing?

Meet the Talking Helpers

Imagine a very important helper in a big country. This person's job is to talk to other countries for their country's leader. It's like a special messenger or a super-duper diplomat! In a country called the United States, there is a very important helper called the Secretary of State. That's a big fancy name, but it just means they are really good at talking to people from other countries. This important helper from the United States thought something wonderful might happen very, very soon. They thought two countries could make a special promise or a plan together. We call this an “agreement” or a “deal.” Think about it like this: you and your friend want to share a yummy snack. You both agree to share it fairly. That's an agreement! It makes everyone happy.

Two Big Countries: US and Iran

Now, let's talk about the two big countries in our news today. One is called the United States, which we just heard about. It's a very big country far away. The other country is called Iran. Iran is also a very big and important country. It’s on a different part of our amazing planet Earth. These two countries have been talking about some important things for a while now. They have big grown-up talks to try and understand each other better and work things out. Did you know? The United States is famous for things like Hollywood movies and big cities. Iran is famous for its ancient history, beautiful art, and delicious food!

The Big News: "Not So Fast!"

So, the special helper from the United States thought that an agreement, a special promise, might happen very, very soon. They even thought it might happen as early as Monday! Monday is a day of the week we all know. It sounds like the agreement was going to happen right away, like when you know you're going to play your favorite game after school today. But then, the country of Iran said something else. They said, "Woah, hold on a minute! Not so fast!" They told everyone that the agreement was "not imminent." That's a big word, isn't it? It just means it's not going to happen super-duper soon, maybe not on Monday, and maybe not even this week.

Why Do Things Take Time?

Sometimes, making big agreements between countries takes a lot of time. It's like when you are building a really tall tower with your blocks. You can't rush it, or it might fall down! Countries have to talk and talk, and then think and think. They want to make sure the agreement is fair for everyone and that it will work well for a long, long time. It’s like when you and your friends are deciding what game to play. Everyone needs to have a say, and sometimes it takes a bit of chatting to pick the perfect game! Did you know? Even though they are far away, what happens between countries like the US and Iran can sometimes affect other countries, too. It’s like when your big brother and sister make a plan, it might change what you get to do later!

Learning to Talk and Listen

This news reminds us how important it is for everyone, big countries and small children, to talk and listen to each other. Even if you don't agree right away, keeping the conversation going is a very good thing. Here in Africa, we also know how important it is for countries to talk together. Our leaders often meet to discuss how to make our continent a better place for all children. So, even though the US and Iran might not have their big agreement on Monday, they are still talking. And that's a good thing! It means they are trying to work things out, little by little. We hope they keep talking and find a way to make a good agreement soon. Because when countries agree, it often helps make the world a more peaceful and happy place for all of us, especially you, our amazing super kids! Thank you for listening to our news report today! See you next time, super friends!
summary: Two big countries, the United States and Iran, are talking about making a special agreement, but Iran says it won't happen as quickly as the US thought.
whyMatters: It's important for countries to talk and make agreements to help keep the world friendly and peaceful, just like it's important for friends to talk to solve problems.

originalTitle: Oil prices slide on hopes of US-Iran peace deal
title: Oil prices slide on hopes of US-Iran peace deal
content: Trump said on Saturday that an agreement would include the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, without giving further details.

originalTitle: She was told to marry in a country which bans girls' education. So she got in a taxi and fled
title: A Super Brave Girl Runs to School!
content:
Hello, Super Learners!

Hi everyone! It's your friend, the news reporter, here! Guess what? We have a very special story for you today. It's about a girl who loves school, just like you! Learning is one of the best adventures, isn't it? You learn to read exciting books. You learn to write your name and cool stories. You learn numbers to count your toys or bananas! And you play with your friends every day. School helps you grow big and smart.

A Special Girl Far Away

Our story is about a brave girl. Let's call her Mali. Mali lived in a country very, very far away. It was not in Africa, but somewhere else in the world. She loved to learn new things. She wanted to go to school every day. Just like you wake up and get ready for school. Mali had big dreams in her heart. She dreamt of being a teacher, maybe even a doctor! She wanted to help people and make the world a better place.

No School for Girls? Oh No!

But in Mali's country, there was a very sad rule. It was a rule that said girls could not go to school. Can you imagine that? No school for girls! It meant no learning, no reading, no writing. No playing with friends in the classroom. No dreaming about being a teacher or a doctor. Just staying at home, all the time. This rule was very unfair. It made many girls, like Mali, feel very sad. Their hearts felt heavy because they could not learn.

Why is School So Super?

Did you know that going to school is like having a superpower? When you learn, you get smarter every day. You learn to solve problems. You learn about the world, about animals, and about plants. School helps you make new friends. It helps you share and be kind to others. **Did you know?** When girls go to school, they grow up to be amazing leaders! They can help their families and their communities. School teaches you how to think big! It opens up a world of possibilities for your future. Every child, boy or girl, should have the chance to learn. It's a right for every single child! **Did you know?** There are millions of children in the world who want to go to school but can't. That's why we must cherish our schools!

A Brave Idea!

Mali's family told her something else sad. They said she should get married. This meant she would never go to school. She would never learn to read or write. Mali felt very, very sad inside. But then, something amazing happened! Mali had a brave idea in her head. She thought, "No! I want to learn! I want to go to school!" She decided she had to be very brave. She decided she would try to find a place where she *could* go to school. Her dream was too important to give up.

Her Big Adventure to Find School

So, one day, Mali did something very daring. She found a taxi. A yellow car that drives people places. She got inside the taxi. She asked the driver to take her far, far away. She was going on an adventure, all by herself. She was leaving her home to find school. It must have been a little bit scary. But Mali was so brave and so determined. She wanted her dream of learning to come true. She travelled for a long, long time. She hoped to find a new place. A place where girls are allowed to learn and play. **Did you know?** Sometimes people have to travel very far to find safety and a better life. This is called fleeing. Mali was fleeing to find her right to learn.

Now She Can Learn!

Guess what? Mali found a safe place! Hooray! She found a country where girls *can* go to school. Now, Mali can go to school every single day. She can learn to read amazing stories. She can write beautiful words and numbers. She can play with new friends. Her heart is full of joy! Her dreams are coming true, one school day at a time. Mali is an example of a super learner and a super brave girl. She never gave up on her dream to learn. **Did you know?** When a girl like Mali gets to go to school, she helps not just herself, but her whole family and even her country! Smart girls make smart communities!

Remembering Other Girls

But this story also makes us a little bit sad. In Mali's old country, many girls are still there. They still cannot go to school. They still cannot learn and play like Mali. They still have to wave goodbye to their dreams. It has been almost five years since girls were banned from school there. Imagine not going to school for five whole years! That's a very, very long time. We hope that one day, all those girls will also be able to go to school. Every girl, everywhere, deserves to learn. **Did you know?** Around the world, there are groups of people working very hard to make sure every child can go to school. They are champions for education!

Why This Story Matters to All of Us!

Why is this story important for *you*, my friends in Africa? Because it reminds us how special school is! You are so lucky to be able to go to school every day. To learn, to play, to grow, to dream big. This story shows us that education is a gift. It's a gift that some children do not have. So, when you go to school tomorrow, think about Mali. Think about how precious learning is. Cherish your teachers. Cherish your books. Cherish your friends. **Did you know?** In many parts of Africa, people are working hard to build more schools and make sure all children, especially girls, get a chance to learn! We celebrate this!

Let's Celebrate Learning!

So, let's cheer for Mali, the brave girl who ran to school! And let's cheer for all of you, super learners in Africa! Keep learning, keep dreaming, and keep being kind. Remember, education is your superpower! You can do amazing things with it. Thank you for listening to this important news today. See you next time, young heroes! This is your friendly news reporter signing off!
summary: A brave girl named Mali fled her home in a far-off country because girls there are not allowed to go to school, and she wanted to learn very much.
whyMatters: This story teaches us that going to school is a very special gift, and every child in the world, boys and girls, should have the chance to learn and make their dreams come true.
